In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} - {{commons category|Category:Kohata Shrine}} [[JA:許波多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631218}} == Japanese content == '''Kohata Shrine'' (Kohata Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) This is Hiding Shrine of ``Kohata Shrine''. In Uji City, there are two shrines named Kōhata Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohata|Q11518385|WD=Q11518385}} and {{ill|lt=Gokasho|Q135477391|WD=Q135477391}}. They are located in the district (However, according to ``Uji Promenade'' Volume 1, Higashi Uji Area Edition, p. 28, the former Kobata includes the current Gokasho), and each has its own shrine history. The former {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} are both {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Formerly known as ``Yanagi Daimyojin''.In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as Meishin Taisha.Kyoto Daijiten, Tankosha, 1984, p399. It is said that Prince Oama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}) and {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} prayed for victory in the war. p28, Kyoto Shimbun, "Encounter with the story: Horses and the tradition of bestowing luck: Kohata Shrine," March 18, 2001. The enshrined deity of both Kōhata Shrines is {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto}} Historical Art Club, “History and Art,” “About Shikinai Taisha Kohata Shrine Sanza,” Shigeru Moriya, 1978, p42. Since Kōhata Shrine is the only Shikinai shrine that enshrines Tenninhomimi no Mikoto as the deity, it is written in {{ill|lt=Shaku Nihongi|Q2710483|WD=Q2710483}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} that ``The deity seated at Kōhata Shrine should be revered as the deity of the shrine, unlike the others.'' In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}), he received the highest rank of Shoichii {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}. After the war, neither of them joined {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}, but affiliated with {{ill|lt=Shinja Honkyo|Q11590707|WD=Q11590707}}``Shinja Honkyo Calendar'' 2016 Edition.{{Citation required|During the Sengoku period, most of the shrine building was destroyed by fire during the war, and the old legend was also lost. |date=August 2017}} ===Kohata Shrine in Gokasho=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Gokasho), keidai-torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii in the shrine grounds | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Gokasho Furukawa 13 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kionoki no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=God Nippon Iwayohiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sangensha Ruzo|1=UNKNOWN}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} | Shinto ritual = Saitan Festival (New Year)
Star Festival (February {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}}) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E}}}} }} In addition to Tennin Homimi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Keirigi no Mikoto}}, the god of Japan Iwayohiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) is enshrined here. According to the shrine biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} enshrined Emperor Kotoku. It is said that |Q317980}} ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a temple to enshrine the imperial ancestors in Yanagiyama, Kuniujigunkohata, Udo District, Yamashiro Province. Because the shrine was located on the land of Yanagiyama, it later came to be called ``Yanagi Daimyojin,'' and its official name was ``Yanagi Shrine'' until the Edo period. During the Meiji period, {{ill|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|Q276535|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}} Uji Gunpowder Factory (currently The entire shrine grounds were taken over as government land for the construction of the {{ill|lt=Ground Self-Defense Force|Q889751|WD=Q889751}}{{ill|lt=Uji Garrison|Q11449523|WD=Q11449523}}), and our company was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location, which was {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}, and at that time the company name was changed to the former Kōhata Shrine.A {{ill|lt=deity statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} of a pair of men and women ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bazu Tenno|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}}), said to have been made in the late Heian period, is said to have been enshrined in the attached temple. The main shrine enshrines a statue of Tenninhomi no Mikoto, said to have been created during the Kamakura period. It is believed that in the past, horse racing rituals were held on the Baba-michi road that runs from the main shrine building to the two towns of East and West, and is said to be the ``Shrine that originated {{ill|lt=horse racing|Q187916|WD=Q187916}}.'' The {{ill|lt=stirrup|Q191657|WD=Q191657}} (stirrup) from the Heian period, which remains as a shrine treasure, is designated as {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. In addition, there is a statue of Bazu Tenno, so it is known as a ``horse shrine'' and is worshiped by horse racing fans and people involved in horse racing. === Kohata Shrine before relocation === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Relocation|1=UNKNOWN}}The previous Kōhata Shrine was located in Yanagiyama (near present-day Obaku Park in Uji City), where it was founded, and the road extending west from there was the approach to Kōhata Shrine. Proceeding west along the road from Obaku Park and beyond the JR and Keihan railroad crossings, there used to be a shrine's Ichinotorii gate, but during the Meiji period, the upper part of the torii gate was removed, and now only the lower part, the Kutsuishi, remains. The remaining stone is also called the ``Kyoto University Moai,'' and is a reminder of the former Kōhata Shrine that was enshrined in Yanagiyama. Additionally, in front of the gymnasium in Obaku Park, where the main shrine is said to have been located before its relocation, the base of the main shrine has now been restored and willows have been planted. === Cultural assets === ;Important cultural property *Main shrine (Sangensha Ruzukuri, Muromachi period) *One pair of Tetsu Hosou Gekujaku Doso Ugan Hanjita Stirrups (Heian period), attached: One pair of Iron tongue long stirrups (Kamakura period) ;Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property *Two male and female divine statues (Bazu Tenno and Benzaiten, Heian period) === Image of Hohata Shrine (Gokasho) === Image:Kohata Shrine_Chinowa.jpg|Kayanowa Image:Kohata shrine1.jpg|Company name stone marker Image:Kohata shrine2.JPG|From the front Image:Kohata shrine3.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine4.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine5.JPG|Torii Image:Kohata shrine8.JPG|Guardian dog (right) Image: Kohata shrine11.JPG | Knob of sacred tree Image:Kohata shrine13.JPG|Place of worship in front of the main shrine Image:Kohata shrine14.JPG|Ema Image:Kohata shrine15.JPG|Shrine office ===Kohata Shrine in Kobata=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Kobata), torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Kobata Higashi Junior High School 1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E}}}} }} {{osm box|w|279530312}} According to the company biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku}} was a dream. It is said that he received a message saying, ``Since I am Tenjin, there will be a tomb for the god in the lower soil, and I will enshrine the spirits there.'' He ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a shrine at Kobata-no-sho. When Prince Oama headed from Otsunomiya to Yoshino ahead of the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, he placed a willow branch at the head of the shrine and prayed for victory, saying, ``If I ascend to the throne, this willow will sprout.'' It is said that when Prince Oama ascended the throne, a willow branch sprouted, and the Emperor gave him the name ``Yanagi Daimyojin'' and donated the shrine's territory in praise of his miraculous powers. It is said to have been enshrined at its current location from Yanagiyama during the Oho era (1161-1163) ``Japan Historical Place Names Daikei Volume 26 Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' Heibonsha, 1981, p227.1908 (ref) “Uji City History 3: Early Modern History and Landscape”, 1975, p457 Table 43. In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} - {{commons category|Category:Kohata Shrine}} [[JA:許波多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631218}} == Japanese content == '''Kohata Shrine'' (Kohata Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) This is Hiding Shrine of ``Kohata Shrine''. In Uji City, there are two shrines named Kōhata Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohata|Q11518385|WD=Q11518385}} and {{ill|lt=Gokasho|Q135477391|WD=Q135477391}}. They are located in the district (However, according to ``Uji Promenade'' Volume 1, Higashi Uji Area Edition, p. 28, the former Kobata includes the current Gokasho), and each has its own shrine history. The former {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} are both {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Formerly known as ``Yanagi Daimyojin''.In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as Meishin Taisha.Kyoto Daijiten, Tankosha, 1984, p399. It is said that Prince Oama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}) and {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} prayed for victory in the war. p28, Kyoto Shimbun, "Encounter with the story: Horses and the tradition of bestowing luck: Kohata Shrine," March 18, 2001. The enshrined deity of both Kōhata Shrines is {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto}} Historical Art Club, “History and Art,” “About Shikinai Taisha Kohata Shrine Sanza,” Shigeru Moriya, 1978, p42. Since Kōhata Shrine is the only Shikinai shrine that enshrines Tenninhomimi no Mikoto as the deity, it is written in {{ill|lt=Shaku Nihongi|Q2710483|WD=Q2710483}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} that ``The deity seated at Kōhata Shrine should be revered as the deity of the shrine, unlike the others.'' In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}), he received the highest rank of Shoichii {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}. After the war, neither of them joined {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}, but affiliated with {{ill|lt=Shinja Honkyo|Q11590707|WD=Q11590707}}``Shinja Honkyo Calendar'' 2016 Edition.{{Citation required|During the Sengoku period, most of the shrine building was destroyed by fire during the war, and the old legend was also lost. |date=August 2017}} ===Kohata Shrine in Gokasho=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Gokasho), keidai-torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii in the shrine grounds | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Gokasho Furukawa 13 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kionoki no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=God Nippon Iwayohiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sangensha Ruzo|1=UNKNOWN}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} | Shinto ritual = Saitan Festival (New Year)
Star Festival (February {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}}) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E}}}} }} In addition to Tennin Homimi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Keirigi no Mikoto}}, the god of Japan Iwayohiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) is enshrined here. According to the shrine biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} enshrined Emperor Kotoku. It is said that |Q317980}} ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a temple to enshrine the imperial ancestors in Yanagiyama, Kuniujigunkohata, Udo District, Yamashiro Province. Because the shrine was located on the land of Yanagiyama, it later came to be called ``Yanagi Daimyojin,'' and its official name was ``Yanagi Shrine'' until the Edo period. During the Meiji period, {{ill|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|Q276535|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}} Uji Gunpowder Factory (currently The entire shrine grounds were taken over as government land for the construction of the {{ill|lt=Ground Self-Defense Force|Q889751|WD=Q889751}}{{ill|lt=Uji Garrison|Q11449523|WD=Q11449523}}), and our company was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location, which was {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}, and at that time the company name was changed to the former Kōhata Shrine.A {{ill|lt=deity statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} of a pair of men and women ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bazu Tenno|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}}), said to have been made in the late Heian period, is said to have been enshrined in the attached temple. The main shrine enshrines a statue of Tenninhomi no Mikoto, said to have been created during the Kamakura period. It is believed that in the past, horse racing rituals were held on the Baba-michi road that runs from the main shrine building to the two towns of East and West, and is said to be the ``Shrine that originated {{ill|lt=horse racing|Q187916|WD=Q187916}}.'' The {{ill|lt=stirrup|Q191657|WD=Q191657}} (stirrup) from the Heian period, which remains as a shrine treasure, is designated as {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. In addition, there is a statue of Bazu Tenno, so it is known as a ``horse shrine'' and is worshiped by horse racing fans and people involved in horse racing. === Kohata Shrine before relocation === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Relocation|1=UNKNOWN}}The previous Kōhata Shrine was located in Yanagiyama (near present-day Obaku Park in Uji City), where it was founded, and the road extending west from there was the approach to Kōhata Shrine. Proceeding west along the road from Obaku Park and beyond the JR and Keihan railroad crossings, there used to be a shrine's Ichinotorii gate, but during the Meiji period, the upper part of the torii gate was removed, and now only the lower part, the Kutsuishi, remains. The remaining stone is also called the ``Kyoto University Moai,'' and is a reminder of the former Kōhata Shrine that was enshrined in Yanagiyama. Additionally, in front of the gymnasium in Obaku Park, where the main shrine is said to have been located before its relocation, the base of the main shrine has now been restored and willows have been planted. === Cultural assets === ;Important cultural property *Main shrine (Sangensha Ruzukuri, Muromachi period) *One pair of Tetsu Hosou Gekujaku Doso Ugan Hanjita Stirrups (Heian period), attached: One pair of Iron tongue long stirrups (Kamakura period) ;Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property *Two male and female divine statues (Bazu Tenno and Benzaiten, Heian period) === Image of Hohata Shrine (Gokasho) === Image:Kohata Shrine_Chinowa.jpg|Kayanowa Image:Kohata shrine1.jpg|Company name stone marker Image:Kohata shrine2.JPG|From the front Image:Kohata shrine3.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine4.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine5.JPG|Torii Image:Kohata shrine8.JPG|Guardian dog (right) Image: Kohata shrine11.JPG | Knob of sacred tree Image:Kohata shrine13.JPG|Place of worship in front of the main shrine Image:Kohata shrine14.JPG|Ema Image:Kohata shrine15.JPG|Shrine office ===Kohata Shrine in Kobata=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Kobata), torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Kobata Higashi Junior High School 1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E}}}} }} {{osm box|w|279530312}} According to the company biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku}} was a dream. It is said that he received a message saying, ``Since I am Tenjin, there will be a tomb for the god in the lower soil, and I will enshrine the spirits there.'' He ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a shrine at Kobata-no-sho. When Prince Oama headed from Otsunomiya to Yoshino ahead of the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, he placed a willow branch at the head of the shrine and prayed for victory, saying, ``If I ascend to the throne, this willow will sprout.'' It is said that when Prince Oama ascended the throne, a willow branch sprouted, and the Emperor gave him the name ``Yanagi Daimyojin'' and donated the shrine's territory in praise of his miraculous powers. It is said to have been enshrined at its current location from Yanagiyama during the Oho era (1161-1163) ``Japan Historical Place Names Daikei Volume 26 Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' Heibonsha, 1981, p227.1908 (ref) “Uji City History 3: Early Modern History and Landscape”, 1975, p457 Table 43. In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} - {{commons category|Category:Kohata Shrine}} [[JA:許波多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631218}} == Japanese content == '''Kohata Shrine'' (Kohata Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) This is Hiding Shrine of ``Kohata Shrine''. In Uji City, there are two shrines named Kōhata Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohata|Q11518385|WD=Q11518385}} and {{ill|lt=Gokasho|Q135477391|WD=Q135477391}}. They are located in the district (However, according to ``Uji Promenade'' Volume 1, Higashi Uji Area Edition, p. 28, the former Kobata includes the current Gokasho), and each has its own shrine history. The former {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} are both {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Formerly known as ``Yanagi Daimyojin''.In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as Meishin Taisha.Kyoto Daijiten, Tankosha, 1984, p399. It is said that Prince Oama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}) and {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} prayed for victory in the war. p28, Kyoto Shimbun, "Encounter with the story: Horses and the tradition of bestowing luck: Kohata Shrine," March 18, 2001. The enshrined deity of both Kōhata Shrines is {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto}} Historical Art Club, “History and Art,” “About Shikinai Taisha Kohata Shrine Sanza,” Shigeru Moriya, 1978, p42. Since Kōhata Shrine is the only Shikinai shrine that enshrines Tenninhomimi no Mikoto as the deity, it is written in {{ill|lt=Shaku Nihongi|Q2710483|WD=Q2710483}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} that ``The deity seated at Kōhata Shrine should be revered as the deity of the shrine, unlike the others.'' In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}), he received the highest rank of Shoichii {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}. After the war, neither of them joined {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}, but affiliated with {{ill|lt=Shinja Honkyo|Q11590707|WD=Q11590707}}``Shinja Honkyo Calendar'' 2016 Edition.{{Citation required|During the Sengoku period, most of the shrine building was destroyed by fire during the war, and the old legend was also lost. |date=August 2017}} ===Kohata Shrine in Gokasho=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Gokasho), keidai-torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii in the shrine grounds | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Gokasho Furukawa 13 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kionoki no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=God Nippon Iwayohiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sangensha Ruzo|1=UNKNOWN}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} | Shinto ritual = Saitan Festival (New Year)
Star Festival (February {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}}) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E}}}} }} In addition to Tennin Homimi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Keirigi no Mikoto}}, the god of Japan Iwayohiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) is enshrined here. According to the shrine biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} enshrined Emperor Kotoku. It is said that |Q317980}} ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a temple to enshrine the imperial ancestors in Yanagiyama, Kuniujigunkohata, Udo District, Yamashiro Province. Because the shrine was located on the land of Yanagiyama, it later came to be called ``Yanagi Daimyojin,'' and its official name was ``Yanagi Shrine'' until the Edo period. During the Meiji period, {{ill|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|Q276535|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}} Uji Gunpowder Factory (currently The entire shrine grounds were taken over as government land for the construction of the {{ill|lt=Ground Self-Defense Force|Q889751|WD=Q889751}}{{ill|lt=Uji Garrison|Q11449523|WD=Q11449523}}), and our company was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location, which was {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}, and at that time the company name was changed to the former Kōhata Shrine.A {{ill|lt=deity statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} of a pair of men and women ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bazu Tenno|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}}), said to have been made in the late Heian period, is said to have been enshrined in the attached temple. The main shrine enshrines a statue of Tenninhomi no Mikoto, said to have been created during the Kamakura period. It is believed that in the past, horse racing rituals were held on the Baba-michi road that runs from the main shrine building to the two towns of East and West, and is said to be the ``Shrine that originated {{ill|lt=horse racing|Q187916|WD=Q187916}}.'' The {{ill|lt=stirrup|Q191657|WD=Q191657}} (stirrup) from the Heian period, which remains as a shrine treasure, is designated as {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. In addition, there is a statue of Bazu Tenno, so it is known as a ``horse shrine'' and is worshiped by horse racing fans and people involved in horse racing. === Kohata Shrine before relocation === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Relocation|1=UNKNOWN}}The previous Kōhata Shrine was located in Yanagiyama (near present-day Obaku Park in Uji City), where it was founded, and the road extending west from there was the approach to Kōhata Shrine. Proceeding west along the road from Obaku Park and beyond the JR and Keihan railroad crossings, there used to be a shrine's Ichinotorii gate, but during the Meiji period, the upper part of the torii gate was removed, and now only the lower part, the Kutsuishi, remains. The remaining stone is also called the ``Kyoto University Moai,'' and is a reminder of the former Kōhata Shrine that was enshrined in Yanagiyama. Additionally, in front of the gymnasium in Obaku Park, where the main shrine is said to have been located before its relocation, the base of the main shrine has now been restored and willows have been planted. === Cultural assets === ;Important cultural property *Main shrine (Sangensha Ruzukuri, Muromachi period) *One pair of Tetsu Hosou Gekujaku Doso Ugan Hanjita Stirrups (Heian period), attached: One pair of Iron tongue long stirrups (Kamakura period) ;Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property *Two male and female divine statues (Bazu Tenno and Benzaiten, Heian period) === Image of Hohata Shrine (Gokasho) === Image:Kohata Shrine_Chinowa.jpg|Kayanowa Image:Kohata shrine1.jpg|Company name stone marker Image:Kohata shrine2.JPG|From the front Image:Kohata shrine3.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine4.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine5.JPG|Torii Image:Kohata shrine8.JPG|Guardian dog (right) Image: Kohata shrine11.JPG | Knob of sacred tree Image:Kohata shrine13.JPG|Place of worship in front of the main shrine Image:Kohata shrine14.JPG|Ema Image:Kohata shrine15.JPG|Shrine office ===Kohata Shrine in Kobata=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Kobata), torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Kobata Higashi Junior High School 1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E}}}} }} {{osm box|w|279530312}} According to the company biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku}} was a dream. It is said that he received a message saying, ``Since I am Tenjin, there will be a tomb for the god in the lower soil, and I will enshrine the spirits there.'' He ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a shrine at Kobata-no-sho. When Prince Oama headed from Otsunomiya to Yoshino ahead of the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, he placed a willow branch at the head of the shrine and prayed for victory, saying, ``If I ascend to the throne, this willow will sprout.'' It is said that when Prince Oama ascended the throne, a willow branch sprouted, and the Emperor gave him the name ``Yanagi Daimyojin'' and donated the shrine's territory in praise of his miraculous powers. It is said to have been enshrined at its current location from Yanagiyama during the Oho era (1161-1163) ``Japan Historical Place Names Daikei Volume 26 Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' Heibonsha, 1981, p227.1908 (ref) “Uji City History 3: Early Modern History and Landscape”, 1975, p457 Table 43. In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} - {{commons category|Category:Kohata Shrine}} [[JA:許波多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631218}} == Japanese content == '''Kohata Shrine'' (Kohata Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) This is Hiding Shrine of ``Kohata Shrine''. In Uji City, there are two shrines named Kōhata Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohata|Q11518385|WD=Q11518385}} and {{ill|lt=Gokasho|Q135477391|WD=Q135477391}}. They are located in the district (However, according to ``Uji Promenade'' Volume 1, Higashi Uji Area Edition, p. 28, the former Kobata includes the current Gokasho), and each has its own shrine history. The former {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} are both {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Formerly known as ``Yanagi Daimyojin''.In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as Meishin Taisha.Kyoto Daijiten, Tankosha, 1984, p399. It is said that Prince Oama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}) and {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} prayed for victory in the war. p28, Kyoto Shimbun, "Encounter with the story: Horses and the tradition of bestowing luck: Kohata Shrine," March 18, 2001. The enshrined deity of both Kōhata Shrines is {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto}} Historical Art Club, “History and Art,” “About Shikinai Taisha Kohata Shrine Sanza,” Shigeru Moriya, 1978, p42. Since Kōhata Shrine is the only Shikinai shrine that enshrines Tenninhomimi no Mikoto as the deity, it is written in {{ill|lt=Shaku Nihongi|Q2710483|WD=Q2710483}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} that ``The deity seated at Kōhata Shrine should be revered as the deity of the shrine, unlike the others.'' In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}), he received the highest rank of Shoichii {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}. After the war, neither of them joined {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}, but affiliated with {{ill|lt=Shinja Honkyo|Q11590707|WD=Q11590707}}``Shinja Honkyo Calendar'' 2016 Edition.{{Citation required|During the Sengoku period, most of the shrine building was destroyed by fire during the war, and the old legend was also lost. |date=August 2017}} ===Kohata Shrine in Gokasho=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Gokasho), keidai-torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii in the shrine grounds | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Gokasho Furukawa 13 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kionoki no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=God Nippon Iwayohiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sangensha Ruzo|1=UNKNOWN}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} | Shinto ritual = Saitan Festival (New Year)
Star Festival (February {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}}) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E}}}} }} In addition to Tennin Homimi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Keirigi no Mikoto}}, the god of Japan Iwayohiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) is enshrined here. According to the shrine biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} enshrined Emperor Kotoku. It is said that |Q317980}} ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a temple to enshrine the imperial ancestors in Yanagiyama, Kuniujigunkohata, Udo District, Yamashiro Province. Because the shrine was located on the land of Yanagiyama, it later came to be called ``Yanagi Daimyojin,'' and its official name was ``Yanagi Shrine'' until the Edo period. During the Meiji period, {{ill|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|Q276535|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}} Uji Gunpowder Factory (currently The entire shrine grounds were taken over as government land for the construction of the {{ill|lt=Ground Self-Defense Force|Q889751|WD=Q889751}}{{ill|lt=Uji Garrison|Q11449523|WD=Q11449523}}), and our company was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location, which was {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}, and at that time the company name was changed to the former Kōhata Shrine.A {{ill|lt=deity statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} of a pair of men and women ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bazu Tenno|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}}), said to have been made in the late Heian period, is said to have been enshrined in the attached temple. The main shrine enshrines a statue of Tenninhomi no Mikoto, said to have been created during the Kamakura period. It is believed that in the past, horse racing rituals were held on the Baba-michi road that runs from the main shrine building to the two towns of East and West, and is said to be the ``Shrine that originated {{ill|lt=horse racing|Q187916|WD=Q187916}}.'' The {{ill|lt=stirrup|Q191657|WD=Q191657}} (stirrup) from the Heian period, which remains as a shrine treasure, is designated as {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. In addition, there is a statue of Bazu Tenno, so it is known as a ``horse shrine'' and is worshiped by horse racing fans and people involved in horse racing. === Kohata Shrine before relocation === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Relocation|1=UNKNOWN}}The previous Kōhata Shrine was located in Yanagiyama (near present-day Obaku Park in Uji City), where it was founded, and the road extending west from there was the approach to Kōhata Shrine. Proceeding west along the road from Obaku Park and beyond the JR and Keihan railroad crossings, there used to be a shrine's Ichinotorii gate, but during the Meiji period, the upper part of the torii gate was removed, and now only the lower part, the Kutsuishi, remains. The remaining stone is also called the ``Kyoto University Moai,'' and is a reminder of the former Kōhata Shrine that was enshrined in Yanagiyama. Additionally, in front of the gymnasium in Obaku Park, where the main shrine is said to have been located before its relocation, the base of the main shrine has now been restored and willows have been planted. === Cultural assets === ;Important cultural property *Main shrine (Sangensha Ruzukuri, Muromachi period) *One pair of Tetsu Hosou Gekujaku Doso Ugan Hanjita Stirrups (Heian period), attached: One pair of Iron tongue long stirrups (Kamakura period) ;Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property *Two male and female divine statues (Bazu Tenno and Benzaiten, Heian period) === Image of Hohata Shrine (Gokasho) === Image:Kohata Shrine_Chinowa.jpg|Kayanowa Image:Kohata shrine1.jpg|Company name stone marker Image:Kohata shrine2.JPG|From the front Image:Kohata shrine3.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine4.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine5.JPG|Torii Image:Kohata shrine8.JPG|Guardian dog (right) Image: Kohata shrine11.JPG | Knob of sacred tree Image:Kohata shrine13.JPG|Place of worship in front of the main shrine Image:Kohata shrine14.JPG|Ema Image:Kohata shrine15.JPG|Shrine office ===Kohata Shrine in Kobata=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Kobata), torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Kobata Higashi Junior High School 1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E}}}} }} {{osm box|w|279530312}} According to the company biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku}} was a dream. It is said that he received a message saying, ``Since I am Tenjin, there will be a tomb for the god in the lower soil, and I will enshrine the spirits there.'' He ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a shrine at Kobata-no-sho. When Prince Oama headed from Otsunomiya to Yoshino ahead of the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, he placed a willow branch at the head of the shrine and prayed for victory, saying, ``If I ascend to the throne, this willow will sprout.'' It is said that when Prince Oama ascended the throne, a willow branch sprouted, and the Emperor gave him the name ``Yanagi Daimyojin'' and donated the shrine's territory in praise of his miraculous powers. It is said to have been enshrined at its current location from Yanagiyama during the Oho era (1161-1163) ``Japan Historical Place Names Daikei Volume 26 Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' Heibonsha, 1981, p227.1908 (ref) “Uji City History 3: Early Modern History and Landscape”, 1975, p457 Table 43. In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} - {{commons category|Category:Kohata Shrine}} [[JA:許波多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631218}} == Japanese content == '''Kohata Shrine'' (Kohata Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) This is Hiding Shrine of ``Kohata Shrine''. In Uji City, there are two shrines named Kōhata Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohata|Q11518385|WD=Q11518385}} and {{ill|lt=Gokasho|Q135477391|WD=Q135477391}}. They are located in the district (However, according to ``Uji Promenade'' Volume 1, Higashi Uji Area Edition, p. 28, the former Kobata includes the current Gokasho), and each has its own shrine history. The former {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} are both {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Formerly known as ``Yanagi Daimyojin''.In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as Meishin Taisha.Kyoto Daijiten, Tankosha, 1984, p399. It is said that Prince Oama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}) and {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} prayed for victory in the war. p28, Kyoto Shimbun, "Encounter with the story: Horses and the tradition of bestowing luck: Kohata Shrine," March 18, 2001. The enshrined deity of both Kōhata Shrines is {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto}} Historical Art Club, “History and Art,” “About Shikinai Taisha Kohata Shrine Sanza,” Shigeru Moriya, 1978, p42. Since Kōhata Shrine is the only Shikinai shrine that enshrines Tenninhomimi no Mikoto as the deity, it is written in {{ill|lt=Shaku Nihongi|Q2710483|WD=Q2710483}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} that ``The deity seated at Kōhata Shrine should be revered as the deity of the shrine, unlike the others.'' In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}), he received the highest rank of Shoichii {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}. After the war, neither of them joined {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}, but affiliated with {{ill|lt=Shinja Honkyo|Q11590707|WD=Q11590707}}``Shinja Honkyo Calendar'' 2016 Edition.{{Citation required|During the Sengoku period, most of the shrine building was destroyed by fire during the war, and the old legend was also lost. |date=August 2017}} ===Kohata Shrine in Gokasho=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Gokasho), keidai-torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii in the shrine grounds | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Gokasho Furukawa 13 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kionoki no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=God Nippon Iwayohiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sangensha Ruzo|1=UNKNOWN}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} | Shinto ritual = Saitan Festival (New Year)
Star Festival (February {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}}) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E}}}} }} In addition to Tennin Homimi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Keirigi no Mikoto}}, the god of Japan Iwayohiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) is enshrined here. According to the shrine biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} enshrined Emperor Kotoku. It is said that |Q317980}} ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a temple to enshrine the imperial ancestors in Yanagiyama, Kuniujigunkohata, Udo District, Yamashiro Province. Because the shrine was located on the land of Yanagiyama, it later came to be called ``Yanagi Daimyojin,'' and its official name was ``Yanagi Shrine'' until the Edo period. During the Meiji period, {{ill|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|Q276535|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}} Uji Gunpowder Factory (currently The entire shrine grounds were taken over as government land for the construction of the {{ill|lt=Ground Self-Defense Force|Q889751|WD=Q889751}}{{ill|lt=Uji Garrison|Q11449523|WD=Q11449523}}), and our company was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location, which was {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}, and at that time the company name was changed to the former Kōhata Shrine.A {{ill|lt=deity statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} of a pair of men and women ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bazu Tenno|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}}), said to have been made in the late Heian period, is said to have been enshrined in the attached temple. The main shrine enshrines a statue of Tenninhomi no Mikoto, said to have been created during the Kamakura period. It is believed that in the past, horse racing rituals were held on the Baba-michi road that runs from the main shrine building to the two towns of East and West, and is said to be the ``Shrine that originated {{ill|lt=horse racing|Q187916|WD=Q187916}}.'' The {{ill|lt=stirrup|Q191657|WD=Q191657}} (stirrup) from the Heian period, which remains as a shrine treasure, is designated as {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. In addition, there is a statue of Bazu Tenno, so it is known as a ``horse shrine'' and is worshiped by horse racing fans and people involved in horse racing. === Kohata Shrine before relocation === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Relocation|1=UNKNOWN}}The previous Kōhata Shrine was located in Yanagiyama (near present-day Obaku Park in Uji City), where it was founded, and the road extending west from there was the approach to Kōhata Shrine. Proceeding west along the road from Obaku Park and beyond the JR and Keihan railroad crossings, there used to be a shrine's Ichinotorii gate, but during the Meiji period, the upper part of the torii gate was removed, and now only the lower part, the Kutsuishi, remains. The remaining stone is also called the ``Kyoto University Moai,'' and is a reminder of the former Kōhata Shrine that was enshrined in Yanagiyama. Additionally, in front of the gymnasium in Obaku Park, where the main shrine is said to have been located before its relocation, the base of the main shrine has now been restored and willows have been planted. === Cultural assets === ;Important cultural property *Main shrine (Sangensha Ruzukuri, Muromachi period) *One pair of Tetsu Hosou Gekujaku Doso Ugan Hanjita Stirrups (Heian period), attached: One pair of Iron tongue long stirrups (Kamakura period) ;Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property *Two male and female divine statues (Bazu Tenno and Benzaiten, Heian period) === Image of Hohata Shrine (Gokasho) === Image:Kohata Shrine_Chinowa.jpg|Kayanowa Image:Kohata shrine1.jpg|Company name stone marker Image:Kohata shrine2.JPG|From the front Image:Kohata shrine3.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine4.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine5.JPG|Torii Image:Kohata shrine8.JPG|Guardian dog (right) Image: Kohata shrine11.JPG | Knob of sacred tree Image:Kohata shrine13.JPG|Place of worship in front of the main shrine Image:Kohata shrine14.JPG|Ema Image:Kohata shrine15.JPG|Shrine office ===Kohata Shrine in Kobata=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Kobata), torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Kobata Higashi Junior High School 1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E}}}} }} {{osm box|w|279530312}} According to the company biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku}} was a dream. It is said that he received a message saying, ``Since I am Tenjin, there will be a tomb for the god in the lower soil, and I will enshrine the spirits there.'' He ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a shrine at Kobata-no-sho. When Prince Oama headed from Otsunomiya to Yoshino ahead of the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, he placed a willow branch at the head of the shrine and prayed for victory, saying, ``If I ascend to the throne, this willow will sprout.'' It is said that when Prince Oama ascended the throne, a willow branch sprouted, and the Emperor gave him the name ``Yanagi Daimyojin'' and donated the shrine's territory in praise of his miraculous powers. It is said to have been enshrined at its current location from Yanagiyama during the Oho era (1161-1163) ``Japan Historical Place Names Daikei Volume 26 Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' Heibonsha, 1981, p227.1908 (ref) “Uji City History 3: Early Modern History and Landscape”, 1975, p457 Table 43. In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} - link|Q11631218}} == Japanese content == '''Kohata Shrine'' (Kohata Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) This is Hiding Shrine of ``Kohata Shrine''. In Uji City, there are two shrines named Kōhata Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohata|Q11518385|WD=Q11518385}} and {{ill|lt=Gokasho|Q135477391|WD=Q135477391}}. They are located in the district (However, according to ``Uji Promenade'' Volume 1, Higashi Uji Area Edition, p. 28, the former Kobata includes the current Gokasho), and each has its own shrine history. The former {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} are both {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Formerly known as ``Yanagi Daimyojin''.In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as Meishin Taisha.Kyoto Daijiten, Tankosha, 1984, p399. It is said that Prince Oama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}) and {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} prayed for victory in the war. p28, Kyoto Shimbun, "Encounter with the story: Horses and the tradition of bestowing luck: Kohata Shrine," March 18, 2001. The enshrined deity of both Kōhata Shrines is {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto}} Historical Art Club, “History and Art,” “About Shikinai Taisha Kohata Shrine Sanza,” Shigeru Moriya, 1978, p42. Since Kōhata Shrine is the only Shikinai shrine that enshrines Tenninhomimi no Mikoto as the deity, it is written in {{ill|lt=Shaku Nihongi|Q2710483|WD=Q2710483}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} that ``The deity seated at Kōhata Shrine should be revered as the deity of the shrine, unlike the others.'' In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}), he received the highest rank of Shoichii {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}. After the war, neither of them joined {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}, but affiliated with {{ill|lt=Shinja Honkyo|Q11590707|WD=Q11590707}}``Shinja Honkyo Calendar'' 2016 Edition.{{Citation required|During the Sengoku period, most of the shrine building was destroyed by fire during the war, and the old legend was also lost. |date=August 2017}} ===Kohata Shrine in Gokasho=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Gokasho), keidai-torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii in the shrine grounds | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Gokasho Furukawa 13 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaninhomi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kionoki no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=God Nippon Iwayohiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sangensha Ruzo|1=UNKNOWN}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} | Shinto ritual = Saitan Festival (New Year)
Star Festival (February {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}}) | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|0|N|135|47|38|E}}}} }} In addition to Tennin Homimi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Keirigi no Mikoto}}, the god of Japan Iwayohiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) is enshrined here. According to the shrine biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} enshrined Emperor Kotoku. It is said that |Q317980}} ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a temple to enshrine the imperial ancestors in Yanagiyama, Kuniujigunkohata, Udo District, Yamashiro Province. Because the shrine was located on the land of Yanagiyama, it later came to be called ``Yanagi Daimyojin,'' and its official name was ``Yanagi Shrine'' until the Edo period. During the Meiji period, {{ill|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|Q276535|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}} Uji Gunpowder Factory (currently The entire shrine grounds were taken over as government land for the construction of the {{ill|lt=Ground Self-Defense Force|Q889751|WD=Q889751}}{{ill|lt=Uji Garrison|Q11449523|WD=Q11449523}}), and our company was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location, which was {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}, and at that time the company name was changed to the former Kōhata Shrine.A {{ill|lt=deity statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} of a pair of men and women ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bazu Tenno|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}}), said to have been made in the late Heian period, is said to have been enshrined in the attached temple. The main shrine enshrines a statue of Tenninhomi no Mikoto, said to have been created during the Kamakura period. It is believed that in the past, horse racing rituals were held on the Baba-michi road that runs from the main shrine building to the two towns of East and West, and is said to be the ``Shrine that originated {{ill|lt=horse racing|Q187916|WD=Q187916}}.'' The {{ill|lt=stirrup|Q191657|WD=Q191657}} (stirrup) from the Heian period, which remains as a shrine treasure, is designated as {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. In addition, there is a statue of Bazu Tenno, so it is known as a ``horse shrine'' and is worshiped by horse racing fans and people involved in horse racing. === Kohata Shrine before relocation === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Relocation|1=UNKNOWN}}The previous Kōhata Shrine was located in Yanagiyama (near present-day Obaku Park in Uji City), where it was founded, and the road extending west from there was the approach to Kōhata Shrine. Proceeding west along the road from Obaku Park and beyond the JR and Keihan railroad crossings, there used to be a shrine's Ichinotorii gate, but during the Meiji period, the upper part of the torii gate was removed, and now only the lower part, the Kutsuishi, remains. The remaining stone is also called the ``Kyoto University Moai,'' and is a reminder of the former Kōhata Shrine that was enshrined in Yanagiyama. Additionally, in front of the gymnasium in Obaku Park, where the main shrine is said to have been located before its relocation, the base of the main shrine has now been restored and willows have been planted. === Cultural assets === ;Important cultural property *Main shrine (Sangensha Ruzukuri, Muromachi period) *One pair of Tetsu Hosou Gekujaku Doso Ugan Hanjita Stirrups (Heian period), attached: One pair of Iron tongue long stirrups (Kamakura period) ;Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property *Two male and female divine statues (Bazu Tenno and Benzaiten, Heian period) === Image of Hohata Shrine (Gokasho) === Image:Kohata Shrine_Chinowa.jpg|Kayanowa Image:Kohata shrine1.jpg|Company name stone marker Image:Kohata shrine2.JPG|From the front Image:Kohata shrine3.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine4.JPG|Chozusha Image:Kohata shrine5.JPG|Torii Image:Kohata shrine8.JPG|Guardian dog (right) Image: Kohata shrine11.JPG | Knob of sacred tree Image:Kohata shrine13.JPG|Place of worship in front of the main shrine Image:Kohata shrine14.JPG|Ema Image:Kohata shrine15.JPG|Shrine office ===Kohata Shrine in Kobata=== {{Shrine | Name=Kohata Shrine | Image=[[File:Kohata-jinja (Kobata), torii.jpg|280px]]
Torii | Location={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Uji City|Q326956|WD=Q326956}}Kobata Higashi Junior High School 1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Meishin University), former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}) | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kohata Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|55|36|N|135|47|55|E}}}} }} {{osm box|w|279530312}} According to the company biography, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|WD=Q1077930}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku}} was a dream. It is said that he received a message saying, ``Since I am Tenjin, there will be a tomb for the god in the lower soil, and I will enshrine the spirits there.'' He ordered {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi Kamatari}} to build a shrine at Kobata-no-sho. When Prince Oama headed from Otsunomiya to Yoshino ahead of the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, he placed a willow branch at the head of the shrine and prayed for victory, saying, ``If I ascend to the throne, this willow will sprout.'' It is said that when Prince Oama ascended the throne, a willow branch sprouted, and the Emperor gave him the name ``Yanagi Daimyojin'' and donated the shrine's territory in praise of his miraculous powers. It is said to have been enshrined at its current location from Yanagiyama during the Oho era (1161-1163) ``Japan Historical Place Names Daikei Volume 26 Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' Heibonsha, 1981, p227.1908 (ref) “Uji City History 3: Early Modern History and Landscape”, 1975, p457 Table 43. In addition, the history of the shrine states, ``Additional note: In January 1907, Tanaka Shrine was enshrined and continued to this day.'' Since Tanaka Shrine in the former Kawara Village was enshrined in , the company's enshrined deities {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu Hikone-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined there. Within the precincts, there is Tomb No. 36 of {{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}} called ``Foxzuka,'' which is said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Mototsune|Q1397196|WD=Q1397196}}. ===Shinuchisha=== *Ichikishima Shrine *Inari Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Amaterasu Grand Shrine *Kasuga Shrine === Okuhata Shrine image (Kohata) === Image: Kasuga Shrine (Shinha Shrine precincts).jpg|Kasuga Shrine Image: Amaterasu Grand Shrine (precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Amaterasu Grand Shrine Image: Hachimangu Shrine (shrine in the precincts of Kōhata Shrine).jpg|Hachimangu Shrine Image: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine (shrines within the precincts of Okohata Shrine).jpg|From left: Atago Shrine, Inari Shrine, Ichikishima Shrine Image:Ujiryo036.jpg|{{ill|lt=Ujiryo|Q38277767|WD=Q38277767}}Tomb No. 36 Image: Camphor Laurel in Kohata shrine 20170826.jpg|Uji City's top 100 famous trees, Kusunoki, height 22m, trunk circumference 3.3m, estimated age 150 years, certified in March 1982 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} ===References=== *Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, “Uji Walkways” Volume 1, Uji East Region, Uji City Cultural Properties Protection Association, 2005 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Yamashiro country|Q11467693|WD=Q11467693}} * {{ill|lt=Kohata Shrine|Q65270949|WD=Q65270949}} -
